nodes.label: Vercel preview deployments block automated testing (SSO)
nodes.rationale: Vercel preview URLs sit behind SSO/auth protection blocking curl/Playwright; recent features were tested post-merge against production by explicit agreement, not pre-merge on preview.
nodes.norm_label: vercel preview deployments block automated testing (sso)

nodes.label: .scanline-overlay scrollbar flicker bug
nodes.rationale: Missing overflow-hidden on .scanline-overlay's container caused scrollHeight to grow in lockstep with its 6s animation; root-caused via direct measurement, not guessed.

nodes.label: Rate-limit testing pollutes shared IPs
nodes.rationale: checkRateLimit() is per-IP (3/hour, Upstash Redis); shared egress IP with a real tester exhausted quota once — check redis.keys before assuming a 429 is a bug.

nodes.label: Mobile Ctrl toolbar iOS dvh bug
nodes.rationale: Passing Playwright's mobile-viewport emulation didn't mean it worked on real hardware; iOS Safari's dynamic viewport chrome miscalculates vh differently. Fixed with position:fixed + dvh + env(safe-area-inset-bottom), confirmed only on actual hardware.
nodes.norm_label: mobile ctrl toolbar ios dvh bug

nodes.label: Feedback issue bodies never contain submitter email
nodes.rationale: Email stored separately in KV (feedback:email:{issueNumber}), reachable only by that exact key; deliberately designed and tested by grepping raw GitHub API responses to confirm zero leakage before shipping.
nodes.norm_label: feedback issue bodies never contain submitter email

nodes.label: Stepwise execution discipline (one confirmed step at a time)
nodes.rationale: Do not chain multiple steps together in one pass, even if they seem safe — a prior unsupervised multi-step run during the docs pipeline work pushed further than intended.
nodes.norm_label: stepwise execution discipline (one confirmed step at a time)

nodes.label: Hard stop: any step that clones CT 180
nodes.norm_label: hard stop: any step that clones ct 180

nodes.label: Hard stop: Step 3.8 full end-to-end test narration
nodes.norm_label: hard stop: step 3.8 full end-to-end test narration

nodes.label: SSH to Proxmox host via Tailscale
nodes.norm_label: ssh to proxmox host via tailscale

nodes.label: controller/ directory placement decision
nodes.rationale: Write the controller into jaysync-lab-playground/controller/, not directly onto CT 105 with no source control, so the running code always corresponds to something committed.

nodes.label: Document as built, not planned principle
nodes.rationale: Don't touch JaySync-Lab's docs (Step 3.9) until the controller is actually working end-to-end — writing docs for a service that doesn't exist yet contradicts the established principle.
nodes.norm_label: document as built, not planned principle

nodes.label: Three real bugs found during Step 3.8 testing
nodes.rationale: A ws_proxy.py handshake crash, a ws_proxy.py disconnect-hang, and a nesting=1 regression in the golden template were found and fixed during real end-to-end testing against the real host.
nodes.norm_label: three real bugs found during step 3.8 testing

nodes.label: Gate 3: Sandboxed test run in a real session clone
nodes.norm_label: gate 3: sandboxed test run in a real session clone

nodes.label: Bash-only submission scope
nodes.rationale: Keeps the review surface small enough for a human to read every submission end to end and matches the sandbox's existing curated-commands-only model rather than opening a general-purpose scripting platform.

nodes.label: Per-contribution retemplate cadence, not batched
nodes.rationale: Batching would let one bad contribution's investigation block every other pending one and make regressions harder to attribute; a slower one-at-a-time cadence costs more operator time but keeps blast radius and attribution contained — opposite of issue #16's tmux fix, which batched because that was internal, already-trusted work.

nodes.label: Playground Phase 3 — Session Controller Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: playground phase 3 — session controller implementation plan

nodes.label: Controller dual-homed network placement (vmbr0 + vmbr_sandbox)
nodes.rationale: vmbr_sandbox has zero routing anywhere by design, so the controller needs a second NIC on vmbr_sandbox to reach session clones' ttyd — making it the one deliberate, narrow bridge between the two networks, matching the original 'controller as single chokepoint' design.

nodes.label: Step 3.2: Create CT 105 as the controller host
nodes.norm_label: step 3.2: create ct 105 as the controller host

nodes.label: Step 3.3: Proxmox resource pool, role, and API token
nodes.norm_label: step 3.3: proxmox resource pool, role, and api token

nodes.label: Step 3.7: Deploy as a systemd service
nodes.norm_label: step 3.7: deploy as a systemd service

nodes.label: Playground Phase 4 — Public Web Interface Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: playground phase 4 — public web interface implementation plan

nodes.label: Ephemeral per-outage email queue notification model
nodes.rationale: Not a persistent mailing list — emails collected while the backend is down are sent once each on recovery, then the queue is fully cleared; no standing list carries over between outages.
nodes.norm_label: ephemeral per-outage email queue notification model

nodes.label: Separate origins via CORS architecture decision
nodes.rationale: Reconsidered from an earlier simpler plan once offline-resilience was required: serving the frontend from CT 105 itself would take it down with the host, defeating the point of a graceful offline state.
nodes.norm_label: separate origins via cors architecture decision

nodes.label: Step 4.3: Core UI (design philosophy)
nodes.rationale: Not a generic dashboard with a terminal bolted on — a real theme, motion, personality. ~60-70% of visitors assumed technical, so UI skips hand-holding (no over-explained tooltips, no excessive onboarding, no dumbed-down copy) while staying concise and confident, not absent, for less experienced visitors.

nodes.label: Cron fallback safety net (daily, Hobby-plan tradeoff)
nodes.rationale: Originally planned hourly, but Vercel's Hobby plan only supports daily Cron; accepted once-daily (0 6 * * *) as a deliberate tradeoff since this is purely a backstop behind the push path, not the primary mechanism — revisit on a paid plan.
nodes.norm_label: cron fallback safety net (daily, hobby-plan tradeoff)

nodes.label: Transition-gating bug fix (send on health-pass, not observed down→up)
nodes.rationale: Originally gated sending on an observed down→up transition marker; found during the 'break the push path' test that a short outage could start and resolve without ever being recorded as down (Cron runs at most daily), leaving a real signup unnotified. Fixed by sending whenever the health check passes and the queue is non-empty.
nodes.norm_label: transition-gating bug fix (send on health-pass, not observed down→up)
